title: eragrostis cilianensis var. major
intro: Eragrostis cilianensis var. major, also known as greater barnyard grass or wood false-bamboo, is a perennial grass species native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Eragrostis cilianensis var. major include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, poultices, and salves for topical application.
